key_insights.insight: Narrative is how we experience ourselves and life, thus story telling is the best form of communication.
key_insights.quote: Narrative is basically you know how we experience ourselves and and life and so as i say in that course if you're not communicating with story as a marketeer you know you're not communicating.

key_insights.insight: People's brains process reality by thinking with their feelings.
key_insights.quote: It's feelings first, story second, the story justifies the feelings.

key_insights.insight: When trying to persuade, understand your target audience's values and how they feel about the world.
key_insights.quote: If you want to tell persuasive stories, you need to first understand exactly who you're communicating with...how they feel about the world, how they feel about themselves, how they feel about, you know, justice and what their values are.

key_insights.insight: Frame your company as a 'light figure' helping the audience (the hero) achieve their goals, not as the hero itself.
key_insights.quote: That's the appropriate position for most companies and organizations and leaders is not to be the hero because your audience feels like they're the hero. You're the light figure. You're there to help them get what they want.

key_insights.insight: The most compelling way to engage an audience is by prioritizing emotion over facts and figures.
key_insights.quote: But the most compelling way to draw them in to whatever we're doing, whether it's a newsletter or a tweet or whatever, is by putting emotion first and really thinking about what the emotion of the content is.

key_insights.insight: Successful persuaders make tribal appeals by aligning with important political goals that resonate with their target audience.
key_insights.quote: One of the big things that the successful kind of persuaders do is to make those tribal appeals.

actionable_advice.advice: Before creating marketing content, identify the core emotions you want to evoke in your audience.
actionable_advice.how_to: Conduct audience research to understand their values, feelings, and the 'tribes' they belong to.

actionable_advice.advice: When pitching or presenting, start with a personal story that connects emotionally with the audience.
actionable_advice.how_to: Share a story about your upbringing, relationship with family, or personal struggles before diving into business details.

actionable_advice.advice: Position your brand as a 'light figure' in your audience's story.
actionable_advice.how_to: Focus marketing messages on how your product or service helps customers achieve their goals and aspirations, rather than boasting about company achievements.

memorable_stories: The speaker opened presentations with a story about his mother, leading to significant business wins.
The Volkswagen ad featuring a snowplow driver using a VW to get to work is cited as a revolutionary example of 'light figure' marketing.
The Nike Colin Kaepernick campaign is presented as a successful example of aligning with political goals.
contrarian_claims: Traditional sales tactics relying on case studies and statistics are ineffective compared to emotionally driven storytelling.
Focusing on facts and figures in marketing is a mistake, emotion is much more important.
Companies should not try to be the 'hero' in their marketing, but instead position themselves as 'light figures' assisting their customers.
episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Moment 101 - This 1 Skill Will Transform Your Life: Will Storr
